title: Mira (Near-Human)
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Otheruses|Mira}} {{Character |type=Jedi |image=[[File:Mira1.jpg]] |name=Mira |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] (13:8 GrS), [[Ruul (moon)|Ruul]]''[[Jedi: Mace Windu]]'' |species=[[Near-Human]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Female]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Black]] |feathers= |eyes=Brown |skin=Pale |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] *[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] |masters=[[Sora Bulq/Legends|Sora Bulq]] |apprentices= }} {{Quote|I—and those for whom I speak—believe that this war represents a failure to listen… We need to stop the fighting and let diplomacy resume.|Mira|}} '''Mira''' was a [[near-Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] who served the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] during the final [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. When the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] broke out between the Republic and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], Mira invoked her [[Right of Denial]] and refused to take up the rank of [[Jedi General/Legends|General]] in the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]]. Representing a group of like-minded Jedi, Mira was killed by [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Asajj Ventress/Legends|Asajj Ventress]] at a conference on [[Ruul (moon)|Ruul]]. ==Biography== [[File:BvV.jpg|thumb|left|Mira witnessing the mock battle between her master and Asajj Ventress just before her death.]] Discovered to be [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] early in life, Mira was taken to the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] for [[Jedi training/Legends|training]] in the ways of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. After [[Initiate Trials/Legends|graduating]] from the [[Jedi academy|Temple's academy]], the respected [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Sora Bulq/Legends|Sora Bulq]] selected Mira as his [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] and together they traveled [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] for several years. While Bulq was a famed [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|lightsaber duelist]], Mira did not demonstrate any prowess with the [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]], instead focusing her energies into maintaining her Jedi calm and her skill in peacekeeping. Eventually, Mira passed her [[Jedi Trials/Legends|Trials of Knighthood]] and was made a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] by the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]]. When the [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] erupted in the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], Jedi Mira invoked the [[Right of Denial]], refusing to return to Coruscant to receive the rank of [[Jedi General/Legends|General]] in the newly formed [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]]. Mira met with like-minded Jedi shortly after the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|Battle]] of [[Geonosis/Legends|Geonosis]] and was selected to represent them when a meeting was called on [[Ruul (moon)|Ruul]]. The meeting, called by Master Bulq and Master [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]], was meant to open negotiations between the High Council and the dissident Jedi in hopes of preventing another [[Jedi Schism|schism]] within the Order. Arriving at her former master's estate, Mira joined fellow dissenters Master [[K'Kruhk]], [[Sian Jeisel]], and Jedi [[Rhad Tarn]] while Masters Windu and Bulq prepared for the meeting. Walking amongst the moon's gardens, Mira felt a disturbance in the Force and rushed to a cliff side to see her former master under attack by the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Asajj Ventress/Legends|Asajj Ventress]]. Unaware that her master was indeed in league with Ventress, for he had fallen to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]] in the weeks leading up to the meeting, Mira leaped down to assist her former mentor. While Ventress and Bulq were in league, the [[Dathomirian/Legends|Dathomirian]] woman did not take orders from the [[Weequay/Legends|Weequay]] and easily overpowered Mira before killing her. Shocked by her sudden death, Bulq carried his former Padawan's corpse up to his estate and mourned over her body. While there, Master Windu confronted Bulq about his betrayal and revealed the Weequay to be in league with Ventress and the Confederacy. ==Personality and traits== Mira was a gentle soul who hated conflict and was determined to settle things peacefully. She was loyal to her former Master [[Sora Bulq/Legends|Sora Bulq]] and attacked [[Asajj Ventress/Legends|Asajj Ventress]] in an attempt to defend him, not realizing that Bulq was actually in league with Ventress. Bulq truly cared about Mira and was upset by her death despite his fall to the dark side. ==Powers and abilities== {{Quote|Mira! Get away! You are no match for her!''"
"''No, she wasn't.|Sora Bulq and Asajj Ventress just as Ventress kills Mira|}} Unlike her Master, Mira was not a skilled lightsaber combatant and was easily slain by Asajj Ventress. Despite her lack of fighting prowess, Mira did display skill with telekinesis by hurling pieces of rubble at Ventress in order to defend Sora Bulq. She also appeared to have a strong bond with her former tutor, able to sense that something was wrong when her former Master was attacked; though she didn't realize that the duel between Bulq and Ventress was staged. ==Behind the scenes== Mira's quote: "This war represents a failure to listen" is the same, word for word, as [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala's]] statement in [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']]. ==Appearances== *''[[Jedi: Mace Windu]]'' {{1st}} ==Sources== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Mira (Near-Human)}} *{{FactFile|116|VEN 1-2|Commander Ventress}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{DB|character|sorabulq|Bulq, Sora}} *''[[Star Wars: The Ultimate Visual Guide: Updated and Expanded]]'' {{Imo}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Mira (Jedi) |hu=Mira (Jedi) |pl=Mira (Jedi) |deGA=1 }} [[Category:Females]] [[Category:Individuals of unidentified species]] [[Category:Near-Humans]] [[Category:Post-Ruusan Jedi Knights]]
